spec_match.rationale: The page probes WebGL context creation with failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat, renderer strings, WebGPU fallback-adapter status, getContext-null handling, and context-loss handling for the Chrome 139 SwiftShader removal.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised the profile, context, fallback, and caveat controls plus both buttons; each branch changed visible decision cards, badges, render output, and the decision log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now frames the Chrome 139 behavior change as explicit WebGL context creation failure and shows the required fallback decision logic.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers hardware success, getContext returning null, software-renderer blocking, Canvas 2D/SVG/message fallbacks, and both manual and profile-driven context loss.

references.rationale: The see-also list includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5166674414927872 plus Chromium's SwiftShader deprecation note and the WebGL context creation section.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, paired foreground/background colors, textContent/DOM construction for dynamic output, and no source inline style attributes.

summary: The previous major issues are closed. DevTools MCP verified live hardware probing, simulated getContext-null fallback, SVG fallback, software-renderer fallback, WebGL1-only probing, manual context-loss fallback, and profile-driven context loss. Console output was clean and all page requests returned 200/204.
